free_text: Resonance analysis.
Full report of 65Antwerp.
Numerical data.
Single level analysis of the resonances in the
total cross section of Pu-239 from 100 eV to 300 eV
Electron Linac.
Booster source of Linac time of flight
spectrometer.
.Two samples of thickness 0.00449 and 0.02477 atoms/b
canned in aluminium.
Time of flight, 120 meter flight path,
4000 channels, 4 mc/s magnetic tape analyser.
Resolution 2.6 nano-sec/meter.
The total cross section is derived from the thicker
sample transmission data between resonance and from
thin sample transmission data for the resonance peaks.
.The total width of each resonance is obtained from
the intersection on the G-n, G-t plane of curves
from the established methods of analysis
area analysis for G-n.
.The transmission area method of Hughes for the
thicker sample, and the minimum transmission
method of Lynn.
Shape analysis was used to determine sigma(0)
and total width, for resonances where Doppler
broadening is small.
- the minimum
transmission method and addition shape analysis
- the transmission
area method
The neutron booster time-of-flight spectrometer
.Background corrected with an equal thickness of
aluminium in beam.
Above 100 eV self shielding effects are negligible.
Nothing said about source of uncertainties
REACTION strings corrected.
SD: Updated to new date formats,lower case.
corrections according last EXFOR rules and Dict. Page
was added to ref. on AERE-PR/NP-9. REL-REF was added.
Derived from the characteristic energy dependence
of the average S and P resonance cross sections.
.From the text on p.2 of EANDC-50-S,1,(98),1967
.From text of EANDC-50-S,1,(98),1967, derived
from total  cross section analysis.
.Sample thickness 0.02477 atoms/b.
From evaluator: 100 times too low. This Subent is the
part of Subent 006, but data are wrong.
